title: Offer Evaluation, Compensation, Negotiation, and Decision Quality · Guided Evidence Sprint
summary: A guided career studio extends evidence-based offer decision model while retaining truthful evidence, accessibility, privacy, security, consent, and learner agency. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of evidence-based offer decision model while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Request and verify a written offer, role, level, reporting line, location, schedule, start, compensation, benefits, contingencies, and decision date.
Compare base, variable pay, equity uncertainty, benefits, leave, hours, commute, equipment, learning, stability, risk, and personal constraints.
Build a source-dated occupation, location, level, and responsibility range while separating public data from this employer decision.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Written Offer
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A verbal offer creates urgency while level, manager, total compensation, contingencies, schedule, intellectual property terms, and decision time remain unclear. The workshop variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Written Offer.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for request and verify a written offer, role, level, reporting line, location, schedule, start, compensation, benefits, contingencies, and decision date. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Written Offer, keep Written Offer connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A verbal offer creates urgency while level, manager, total compensation, contingencies, schedule, intellectual property terms, and decision time remain unclear. The workshop variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Written Offer condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: GitHub Healthy Contributions Guidance — Controls README, contribution, conduct, license, support, governance, issue-template, and stewardship signals without equating activity volume with quality.

For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Written Offer, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: A verbal promise is equivalent to an enforceable final agreement.

  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Decision Record
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Decision Record: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A verbal offer creates urgency while level, manager, total compensation, contingencies, schedule, intellectual property terms, and decision time remain unclear. The workshop variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Document benefits, risks, unknowns, pressure, advice boundaries, decline conditions, acceptance terms, and the learner-owned reasoned decision.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Decision Record.
steps.why: Retrieving Decision Record through the concrete evidence-based offer decision model case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Decision Record, keep Decision Record connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Document benefits, risks, unknowns, pressure, advice boundaries, decline conditions, acceptance terms, and the learner-owned reasoned decision.

hints: For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Decision Record, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Decision Record precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the transferred model · Workshop Decision Record, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A verbal offer creates urgency while level, manager, total compensation, contingencies, schedule, intellectual property terms, and decision time remain unclear. The workshop variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Document benefits, risks, unknowns, pressure, advice boundaries, decline conditions, acceptance terms, and the learner-owned reasoned decision.

For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Decision Record, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: Accepting quickly demonstrates commitment and protects the offer.
